hero.title: Hermit
hero.subtitle: An enterprise-grade workbench for AI agent team collaboration and management. Manage digital employees like a real engineering organization: teams, roles, tasks, messages, reviews, logs, and knowledge assets in one local-first control plane.

features.title: AI Engineering Teams
features.description: Create a lead and multiple persistent members with roles, workflows, inboxes, and runtimes.

features.title: Kanban Workflow
features.description: Turn requests into visible tasks with owners, comments, state transitions, review, and completion.

features.title: Task-level Code Review
features.description: Review diffs per task, accept or reject hunks, and keep delivery evidence close to the work.

features.title: Member Messaging
features.description: Coordinate through inboxes, task comments, direct messages, and cross-team links.

features.title: Execution Logs
features.description: Inspect tool calls, process state, startup diagnostics, permission blocks, and runtime output.

features.title: Skills and Team Templates
features.description: Version roles, review standards, SOPs, and reusable team patterns through Git-backed sources.

faq.question: What is Hermit?
faq.answer: Hermit is a local-first AI engineering team workbench. It currently defaults to the official Claude Code / claude CLI and wraps it with teams, kanban, messaging, review, and logs.

faq.question: Is it free?
faq.answer: Hermit itself is free and open source under AGPL-3.0. You still need access to the runtime you choose, such as Claude Code today or other adapters in the future.

faq.question: Does Hermit host my code?
faq.answer: No. Hermit runs on your machine, reads your selected project folders and local Claude data, and does not host your core code.

faq.question: Which runtimes are supported?
faq.answer: The main path targets the official Claude Code / claude CLI. A Cursor CLI adapter foundation exists for headless and solo exploration; do not assume OpenCode, Codex, or Gemini runtimes are available by default.

faq.question: Why can Windows feel slower?
faq.answer: Windows file scanning, antivirus, and CLI startup can slow first paint and teammate bootstrap. Hermit uses smaller launch batches, a wider grace window, and deferred watchers to reduce false failures.

faq.question: How will teams and skills be shared?
faq.answer: The product direction is Git-backed skill and team-template sources, so organizational knowledge can be reviewed, versioned, reused, and rolled back like code.

download.title: Download Hermit
download.note: Choose your platform and start managing a local AI engineering team.

testimonials.name: Engineering Lead
testimonials.text: Instead of one giant chat, I can split work into tasks, assign agents, review diffs, and keep the whole process visible.

testimonials.name: Indie Builder
testimonials.text: Even solo mode is useful: the board, logs, and review flow make long-running agent work much easier to control.

testimonials.name: Platform Engineer
testimonials.text: The logs and member diagnostics are the difference between hoping an agent works and actually knowing what happened.

testimonials.name: Architect
testimonials.text: Hermit focuses on reusable team assets instead of becoming yet another model gateway. That is the right layer.

pricing.name: Open Source
pricing.description: Local-first and open source. Bring your own Claude Code or other supported runtime access.
